(\n\tstateKey framework.StateKey = Name\n\n\tmaxUtilization = 100\n)\n\n// the state is initialized in PreFilter phase. because we save the pointer in\n// framework.CycleState, in the later phases we don't need to call Write method\n// to update the value\ntype stateData struct {\n\tallBound bool\n\t// podVolumesByNode holds the pod's volume information found in the Filter\n\t// phase for each node\n\t// it's initialized in the PreFilter phase\n\tpodVolumesByNode map[string]*PodVolumes\n\tpodVolumeClaims  *PodVolumeClaims\n\t// hasStaticBindings declares whether the pod contains one or more StaticBinding.\n\t// If not, vloumeBinding will skip score extension point.\n\thasStaticBindings bool\n\tsync.Mutex\n}\n\nfunc (d *stateData) Clone() framework.StateData {\n\treturn d\n}\n\n// VolumeBinding is a plugin that binds pod volumes in scheduling.\n// In the Filter phase, pod binding cache is created for the pod and used in\n// Reserve and PreBind phases.\ntype VolumeBinding struct {\n\tBinder    SchedulerVolumeBinder\n\tPVCLister corelisters.PersistentVolumeClaimLister\n\tscorer    volumeCapacityScorer\n\tfts       feature.Features\n}\n\nvar _ framework.PreFilterPlugin = &VolumeBinding{}\nvar _ framework.FilterPlugin = &VolumeBinding{}\nvar _ framework.ReservePlugin = &VolumeBinding{}\nvar _ framework.PreBindPlugin = &VolumeBinding{}\nvar _ framework.PreScorePlugin = &VolumeBinding{}\nvar _ framework.ScorePlugin = &VolumeBinding{}\nvar _ framework.EnqueueExtensions = &VolumeBinding{}\n\n// Name is the name of the plugin used in Registry and configurations.\nconst Name = names.VolumeBinding\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin. It is used in logs, etc.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) Name() string {\n\treturn Name\n}\n\n// EventsToRegister returns the possible events that may make a Pod\n// failed by this plugin schedulable.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) EventsToRegister() []framework.ClusterEventWithHint {\n\tevents := []framework.ClusterEventWithHint{\n\t\t// Pods may fail because of missing or mis-configured storage class\n\t\t// (e.g., allowedTopologies, volumeBindingMode), and hence may become\n\t\t// schedulable upon StorageClass Add or Update events.\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.StorageClass, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\n\t\t// We bind PVCs with PVs, so any changes may make the pods schedulable.\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.PersistentVolumeClaim, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.PersistentVolume, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\n\t\t// Pods may fail to find available PVs because the node labels do not\n\t\t// match the storage class's allowed topologies or PV's node affinity.\n\t\t// A new or updated node may make pods schedulable.\n\t\t//\n\t\t// A note about UpdateNodeTaint event:\n\t\t// NodeAdd QueueingHint isn't always called because of the internal feature called preCheck.\n\t\t// As a common problematic scenario,\n\t\t// when a node is added but not ready, NodeAdd event is filtered out by preCheck and doesn't arrive.\n\t\t// In such cases, this plugin may miss some events that actually make pods schedulable.\n\t\t// As a workaround, we add UpdateNodeTaint event to catch the case.\n\t\t// We can remove UpdateNodeTaint when we remove the preCheck feature.\n\t\t// See: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/110175\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.UpdateNodeLabel | framework.UpdateNodeTaint}},\n\t\t// We rely on CSI node to translate in-tree PV to CSI.\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.CSINode, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\n\t\t// When CSIStorageCapacity is enabled, pods may become schedulable\n\t\t// on CSI driver & storage capacity changes.\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.CSIDriver, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.CSIStorageCapacity, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}},\n\t}\n\treturn events\n}\n\n// podHasPVCs returns 2 values:\n// - the first one to denote if the given \"pod\" has any PVC defined.\n// - the second one to return any error if the requested PVC is illegal.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) podHasPVCs(pod *v1.Pod) (bool, error) {\n\thasPVC := false\n\tfor _, vol := range pod.Spec.Volumes {\n\t\tvar pvcName string\n\t\tisEphemeral := false\n\t\tswitch {\n\t\tcase vol.PersistentVolumeClaim != nil:\n\t\t\tpvcName = vol.PersistentVolumeClaim.ClaimName\n\t\tcase vol.Ephemeral != nil:\n\t\t\tpvcName = ephemeral.VolumeClaimName(pod, &vol)\n\t\t\tisEphemeral = true\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t// Volume is not using a PVC, ignore\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\thasPVC = true\n\t\tpvc, err := pl.PVCLister.PersistentVolumeClaims(pod.Namespace).Get(pvcName)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t// The error usually has already enough context (\"persistentvolumeclaim \"myclaim\" not found\"),\n\t\t\t// but we can do better for generic ephemeral inline volumes where that situation\n\t\t\t// is normal directly after creating a pod.\n\t\t\tif isEphemeral && apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {\n\t\t\t\terr = fmt.Errorf(\"waiting for ephemeral volume controller to create the persistentvolumeclaim %q\", pvcName)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn hasPVC, err\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif pvc.Status.Phase == v1.ClaimLost {\n\t\t\treturn hasPVC, fmt.Errorf(\"persistentvolumeclaim %q bound to non-existent persistentvolume %q\", pvc.Name, pvc.Spec.VolumeName)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif pvc.DeletionTimestamp != nil {\n\t\t\treturn hasPVC, fmt.Errorf(\"persistentvolumeclaim %q is being deleted\", pvc.Name)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif isEphemeral {\n\t\t\tif err := ephemeral.VolumeIsForPod(pod, pvc); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn hasPVC, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn hasPVC, nil\n}\n\n// PreFilter invoked at the prefilter extension point to check if pod has all\n// immediate PVCs bound. If not all immediate PVCs are bound, an\n// UnschedulableAndUnresolvable is returned.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) PreFilter(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (*framework.PreFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\n\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n\t// If pod does not reference any PVC, we don't need to do anything.\n\tif hasPVC, err := pl.podHasPVCs(pod); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, err.Error())\n\t} else if !hasPVC {\n\t\tstate.Write(stateKey, &stateData{})\n\t\treturn nil, framework.NewStatus(framework.Skip)\n\t}\n\tpodVolumeClaims, err := pl.Binder.GetPodVolumeClaims(logger, pod)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\tif len(podVolumeClaims.unboundClaimsImmediate) > 0 {\n\t\t// Return UnschedulableAndUnresolvable error if immediate claims are\n\t\t// not bound. Pod will be moved to active/backoff queues once these\n\t\t// claims are bound by PV controller.\n\t\tstatus := framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable)\n\t\tstatus.AppendReason(\"pod has unbound immediate PersistentVolumeClaims\")\n\t\treturn nil, status\n\t}\n\t// Attempt to reduce down the number of nodes to consider in subsequent scheduling stages if pod has bound claims.\n\tvar result *framework.PreFilterResult\n\tif eligibleNodes := pl.Binder.GetEligibleNodes(logger, podVolumeClaims.boundClaims); eligibleNodes != nil {\n\t\tresult = &framework.PreFilterResult{\n\t\t\tNodeNames: eligibleNodes,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tstate.Write(stateKey, &stateData{\n\t\tpodVolumesByNode: make(map[string]*PodVolumes),\n\t\tpodVolumeClaims: &PodVolumeClaims{\n\t\t\tboundClaims:                podVolumeClaims.boundClaims,\n\t\t\tunboundClaimsDelayBinding:  podVolumeClaims.unboundClaimsDelayBinding,\n\t\t\tunboundVolumesDelayBinding: podVolumeClaims.unboundVolumesDelayBinding,\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\n// PreFilterExtensions returns prefilter extensions, pod add and remove.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) PreFilterExtensions() framework.PreFilterExtensions {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc getStateData(cs *framework.CycleState) (*stateData, error) {\n\tstate, err := cs.Read(stateKey)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\ts, ok := state.(*stateData)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"unable to convert state into stateData\")\n\t}\n\treturn s, nil\n}\n\n// Filter invoked at the filter extension point.\n// It evaluates if a pod can fit due to the volumes it requests,\n// for both bound and unbound PVCs.\n//\n// For PVCs that are bound, then it checks that the corresponding PV's node affinity is\n// satisfied by the given node.\n//\n// For PVCs that are unbound, it tries to find available PVs that can satisfy the PVC requirements\n// and that the PV node affinity is satisfied by the given node.\n//\n// If storage capacity tracking is enabled, then enough space has to be available\n// for the node and volumes that still need to be created.\n//\n// The predicate returns true if all bound PVCs have compatible PVs with the node, and if all unbound\n// PVCs can be matched with an available and node-compatible PV.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) Filter(ctx context.Context, cs *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n\tnode := nodeInfo.Node()\n\n\tstate, err := getStateData(cs)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\n\tpodVolumes, reasons, err := pl.Binder.FindPodVolumes(logger, pod, state.podVolumeClaims, node)\n\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\n\tif len(reasons) > 0 {\n\t\tstatus := framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable)\n\t\tfor _, reason := range reasons {\n\t\t\tstatus.AppendReason(string(reason))\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn status\n\t}\n\n\t// multiple goroutines call `Filter` on different nodes simultaneously and the `CycleState` may be duplicated, so we must use a local lock here\n\tstate.Lock()\n\tstate.podVolumesByNode[node.Name] = podVolumes\n\tstate.hasStaticBindings = state.hasStaticBindings || (podVolumes != nil && len(podVolumes.StaticBindings) > 0)\n\tstate.Unlock()\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// PreScore invoked at the preScore extension point. It checks whether volumeBinding can skip Score\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) PreScore(ctx context.Context, cs *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\tif pl.scorer == nil {\n\t\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Skip)\n\t}\n\tstate, err := getStateData(cs)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\tif state.hasStaticBindings {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Skip)\n}\n\n// Score invoked at the score extension point.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) Score(ctx context.Context, cs *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\n\tif pl.scorer == nil {\n\t\treturn 0, nil\n\t}\n\tstate, err := getStateData(cs)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\tpodVolumes, ok := state.podVolumesByNode[nodeName]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn 0, nil\n\t}\n\t// group by storage class\n\tclassResources := make(classResourceMap)\n\tfor _, staticBinding := range podVolumes.StaticBindings {\n\t\tclass := staticBinding.StorageClassName()\n\t\tstorageResource := staticBinding.StorageResource()\n\t\tif _, ok := classResources[class]; !ok {\n\t\t\tclassResources[class] = &StorageResource{\n\t\t\t\tRequested: 0,\n\t\t\t\tCapacity:  0,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tclassResources[class].Requested += storageResource.Requested\n\t\tclassResources[class].Capacity += storageResource.Capacity\n\t}\n\treturn pl.scorer(classResources), nil\n}\n\n// ScoreExtensions of the Score plugin.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) ScoreExtensions() framework.ScoreExtensions {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Reserve reserves volumes of pod and saves binding status in cycle state.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) Reserve(ctx context.Context, cs *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) *framework.Status {\n\tstate, err := getStateData(cs)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\t// we don't need to hold the lock as only one node will be reserved for the given pod\n\tpodVolumes, ok := state.podVolumesByNode[nodeName]\n\tif ok {\n\t\tallBound, err := pl.Binder.AssumePodVolumes(klog.FromContext(ctx), pod, nodeName, podVolumes)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tstate.allBound = allBound\n\t} else {\n\t\t// may not exist if the pod does not reference any PVC\n\t\tstate.allBound = true\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// PreBind will make the API update with the assumed bindings and wait until\n// the PV controller has completely finished the binding operation.\n//\n// If binding errors, times out or gets undone, then an error will be returned to\n// retry scheduling.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) PreBind(ctx context.Context, cs *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) *framework.Status {\n\ts, err := getStateData(cs)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\tif s.allBound {\n\t\t// no need to bind volumes\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\t// we don't need to hold the lock as only one node will be pre-bound for the given pod\n\tpodVolumes, ok := s.podVolumesByNode[nodeName]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"no pod volumes found for node %q\", nodeName))\n\t}\n\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"Trying to bind volumes for pod\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod))\n\terr = pl.Binder.BindPodVolumes(ctx, pod, podVolumes)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"Failed to bind volumes for pod\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"err\", err)\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"Success binding volumes for pod\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod))\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Unreserve clears assumed PV and PVC cache.\n// It's idempotent, and does nothing if no cache found for the given pod.\nfunc (pl *VolumeBinding) Unreserve(ctx context.Context, cs *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) {\n\ts, err := getStateData(cs)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\t// we don't need to hold the lock as only one node may be unreserved\n\tpodVolumes, ok := s.podVolumesByNode[nodeName]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tpl.Binder.RevertAssumedPodVolumes(podVolumes)\n}\n\n// New initializes a new plugin and returns it.\nfunc New(ctx context.Context, plArgs runtime.Object, fh framework.Handle, fts fts.EnableVolumeCapacityPriority,\n\t}); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tpodInformer := fh.SharedInformerFactory().Core().V1().Pods()\n\tnodeInformer := fh.SharedInformerFactory().Core().V1().Nodes()\n\tpvcInformer := fh.SharedInformerFactory().Core().V1().PersistentVolumeClaims()\n\tpvInformer := fh.SharedInformerFactory().Core().V1().PersistentVolumes()\n\tstorageClassInformer := fh.SharedInformerFactory().Storage().V1().StorageClasses()\n\tcsiNodeInformer := fh.SharedInformerFactory().Storage().V1().CSIDrivers(),\n\t\tCSIStorageCapacityInformer: fh.SharedInformerFactory().Storage().V1().CSIStorageCapacities(),\n\t}\n\tbinder := that.\n\tpodRequests []int64\n}\n\n// Clone implements the mandatory Clone interface. We don't really copy the data since\n// there is no need for that.\nfunc (s *balancedAllocationPreScoreState) Clone() framework.StateData {\n\treturn s\n}\n\n// PreScore calculates incoming pod's resource requests and writes them to the cycle state used.\nfunc (ba *BalancedAllocation) PreScore(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\tstate := &balancedAllocationPreScoreState{\n\t\tpodRequests: ba.calculatePodResourceRequestList(pod, ba.resources),\n\t}\n\tcycleState.Write(balancedAllocationPreScoreStateKey, state)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc getBalancedAllocationPreScoreState(cycleState *framework.CycleState) (*balancedAllocationPreScoreState, error) {\n\tc, err := cycleState.Read(balancedAllocationPreScoreStateKey)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"reading %q from cycleState: %w\", balancedAllocationPreScoreStateKey, err)\n\t}\n\n\ts, ok := c.(*balancedAllocationPreScoreState)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid PreScore state, got type %T\", c)\n\t}\n\treturn s, nil\n}\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin. It is used in logs, etc.\nfunc (ba *BalancedAllocation) Name() string {\n\treturn BalancedAllocationName\n}\n\n// Score invoked at the score extension point.\nfunc (ba *BalancedAllocation) Score(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\n\tnodeInfo, err := ba.handle.SnapshotSharedLister().NodeInfos().Get(nodeName)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"getting node %q from Snapshot: %w\", nodeName, err))\n\t}\n\n\ts, err := getBalancedAllocationPreScoreState(state)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\ts = &balancedAllocationPreScoreState{podRequests: ba.calculatePodResourceRequestList(pod, ba.resources)}\n\t}\n\n\t// ba.score favors nodes with balanced resource usage rate.\n\t// It calculates the standard deviation for those resources and prioritizes the node based on how close the usage of those resources is to each other.\n\t// Detail: score = (1 - std) * MaxNodeScore, where std is calculated by the root square of Σ((fraction(i)-mean)^2)/len(resources)\n\t// The algorithm is partly inspired by:\n\t// \"Wei Huang et al. An Energy Efficient Virtual Machine Placement Algorithm with Balanced Resource Utilization\"\n\treturn ba.score(ctx, pod, nodeInfo, s.podRequests)\n}\n\n// ScoreExtensions of the Score plugin.\nfunc (ba *BalancedAllocation) ScoreExtensions() framework.ScoreExtensions {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// NewBalancedAllocation initializes a new plugin and returns it.\nfunc NewBalancedAllocation(_ context.Context, baArgs runtime.Object, h framework.Handle, fts feature.Features) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n\targs, ok := baArgs.(*config.NodeResourcesBalancedAllocationArgs)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"want args to be of type NodeResourcesBalancedAllocationArgs, got %T\", baArgs)\n\t}\n\n\tif err := validation.ValidateNodeResourcesBalancedAllocationArgs(nil, args); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\treturn &BalancedAllocation{\n\t\thandle: h,\n\t\tresourceAllocationScorer: resourceAllocationScorer{\n\t\t\tName:         BalancedAllocationName,\n\t\t\tscorer:       balancedResourceScorer,\n\t\t\tuseRequested: true,\n\t\t\tresources:    args.Resources,\n\t\t},\n\t}, nil\n}\n\nfunc balancedResourceScorer(requested, allocable []int64) int64 {\n\tvar resourceToFractions []float64\n\tvar totalFraction float64\n\tfor i := range requested {\n\t\tif allocable[i] == 0 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tfraction := float64(requested[i]) / float64(allocable[i])\n\t\tif fraction > 1 {\n\t\t\tfraction = 1\n\t\t}\n\t\ttotalFraction += fraction\n\t\tresourceToFractions = append(resourceToFractions, fraction)\n\t}\n\n\tstd := 0.0\n\n\t// For most cases, resources are limited to cpu and memory, the std could be simplified to std := (fraction1-fraction2)/2\n\t// len(fractions) > 2: calculate std based on the well-known formula - root square of Σ((fraction(i)-mean)^2)/len(fractions)\n\t// Otherwise, set the std to zero is enough.\n\tif len(resourceToFractions) == 2 {\n\t\tstd = math.Abs((resourceToFractions[0] - resourceToFractions[1]) / 2)\n\n\t} else if len(resourceToFractions) > 2 {\n\t\tmean := totalFraction / float64(len(resourceToFractions))\n\t\tvar sum float64\n\t\tfor _, fraction := range resourceToFractions {\n\t\t\tsum = sum + (fraction-mean)*(fraction-mean)\n\t\t}\n\t\tstd = math.Sqrt(sum / float64(len(resourceToFractions)))\n\t}\n\n\t// STD (standard deviation) is always a positive value. 1-deviation lets the score to be higher for node which has least deviation and\n\t// multiplying it with `MaxNodeScore` provides the scaling factor needed.\n\treturn int64((1 - std) * float64(framework.MaxNodeScore))\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":108,"oldLines":8,"newStart":108,"newLines":8,"lines":["   return ba.score(ctx, pod, nodeInfo, s.podRequests)"," }"," ","-// ScoreExtensions of the Score plugin.","-func (ba *BalancedAllocation) ScoreExtensions() framework.ScoreExtensions {","+// ScoreNormalizer of the Score plugin.","+func (ba *BalancedAllocation) ScoreNormalizer() framework.ScoreNormalizer {","   return nil"," }"," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{\n\treturn s\n}\n\n// preScoreState computed at PreScore and used at Score.\ntype preScoreState struct {\n\t// podRequests have the same order as the resources defined in NodeResourcesBalancedAllocationArgs.Resources,\n\t// same for other place we store a list like that.\n\tpodRequests []int64\n}\n\n// Clone implements the mandatory Clone interface. We don't really copy the data since\n// there is no need for that.\nfunc (s *preScoreState) Clone() framework.StateData {\n\treturn s\n}\n\n// PreScore calculates incoming pod's resource requests and writes them to the cycle state used.\nfunc (f *Fit) PreScore(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\tstate := &preScoreState{\n\t\tpodRequests: f.calculatePodResourceRequestList(pod, f.resources),\n\t}\n\tcycleState.Write(preScoreStateKey, state)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc getPreScoreState(cycleState *framework.CycleState) (*preScoreState, error) {\n\tc, err := cycleState.Read(preScoreStateKey)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"reading %q from cycleState: %w\", preScoreStateKey, err)\n\t}\n\n\ts, ok := c.(*preScoreState)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid PreScore state, got type %T\", c)\n\t}\n\treturn s, nil\n}\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin. It is used in logs, etc.\nfunc (f *Fit) Name() string {\n\treturn Name\n}\n\n// NewFit initializes a new plugin and returns it.\nfunc NewFit(_ context.Context, plArgs runtime.Object, h framework.Handle, fts feature.Features) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n\targs, ok := plArgs.(*config.NodeResourcesFitArgs)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"want args to be of type NodeResourcesFitArgs, got %T\", plArgs)\n\t}\n\tif err := validation.ValidateNodeResourcesFitArgs(nil, args); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tif args.ScoringStrategy == nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"scoring strategy not specified\")\n\t}\n\n\tstrategy := args.ScoringStrategy.Type\n\tscorePlugin, exists := nodeResourceStrategyTypeMap[strategy]\n\tif !exists {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"scoring strategy %s is not supported\", strategy)\n\t}\n\n\treturn &Fit{\n\t\tignoredResources:                sets.New(args.IgnoredResources...),\n\t\tignoredResourceGroups:           sets.New(args.IgnoredResourceGroups...),\n\t\tenableInPlacePodVerticalScaling: fts.EnableInPlacePodVerticalScaling,\n\t\tenableSidecarContainers:         fts.EnableSidecarContainers,\n\t\thandle:                          h,\n\t\tresourceAllocationScorer:        *scorePlugin(args),\n\t}, nil\n}\n\n// computePodResourceRequest returns a framework.Resource that covers the largest\n// width in each resource dimension. Because init-containers run sequentially, we collect\n// the max in each dimension iteratively. In contrast, we sum the resource vectors for\n// regular containers since they run simultaneously.\n//\n// # The resources defined for Overhead should be added to the calculated Resource request sum\n//\n// Example:\n//\n// Pod:\n//\n//\tInitContainers\n//\t  IC1:\n//\t    CPU: 2\n//\t    Memory: 1G\n//\t  IC2:\n//\t    CPU: 2\n//\t    Memory: 3G\n//\tContainers\n//\t  C1:\n//\t    CPU: 2\n//\t    Memory: 1G\n//\t  C2:\n//\t    CPU: 1\n//\t    Memory: 1G\n//\n// Result: CPU: 3, Memory: 3G\nfunc computePodResourceRequest(pod *v1.Pod) *preFilterState {\n\t// pod hasn't scheduled yet so we don't need to worry about InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled\n\treqs := resource.PodRequests(pod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{})\n\tresult := &preFilterState{}\n\tresult.SetMaxResource(reqs)\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// PreFilter invoked at the prefilter extension point.\nfunc (f *Fit) PreFilter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (*framework.PreFilterResult, *framework.Status) {\n\tif !f.enableSidecarContainers && hasRestartableInitContainer(pod) {\n\t\t// Scheduler will calculate resources usage for a Pod containing\n\t\t// restartable init containers that will be equal or more than kubelet will\n\t\t// require to run the Pod. So there will be no overbooking. However, to\n\t\t// avoid the inconsistency in resource calculation between the scheduler\n\t\t// and the older (before v1.28) kubelet, make the Pod unschedulable.\n\t\treturn nil, framework.NewStatus(framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable, \"Pod has a restartable init container and the SidecarContainers feature is disabled\")\n\t}\n\tcycleState.Write(preFilterStateKey, computePodResourceRequest(pod))\n\treturn nil, nil\n}\n\n// PreFilterExtensions returns prefilter extensions, pod add and remove.\nfunc (f *Fit) PreFilterExtensions() framework.PreFilterExtensions {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc getPreFilterState(cycleState *framework.CycleState) (*preFilterState, error) {\n\tc, err := cycleState.Read(preFilterStateKey)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// preFilterState doesn't exist, likely PreFilter wasn't invoked.\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error reading %q from cycleState: %w\", preFilterStateKey, err)\n\t}\n\n\ts, ok := c.(*preFilterState)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"%+v  convert to NodeResourcesFit.preFilterState error\", c)\n\t}\n\treturn s, nil\n}\n\n// EventsToRegister returns the possible events that may make a Pod\n// failed by this plugin schedulable.\nfunc (f *Fit) EventsToRegister() []framework.ClusterEventWithHint {\n\tpodActionType := framework.Delete\n\tif f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling {\n\t\t// If InPlacePodVerticalScaling (KEP 1287) is enabled, then PodUpdate event should be registered\n\t\t// for this plugin since a Pod update may free up resources that make other Pods schedulable.\n\t\tpodActionType |= framework.Update\n\t}\n\treturn []framework.ClusterEventWithHint{\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Pod, ActionType: podActionType}, QueueingHintFn: f.isSchedulableAfterPodChange},\n\t\t{Event: framework.ClusterEvent{Resource: framework.Node, ActionType: framework.Add | framework.Update}, QueueingHintFn: f.isSchedulableAfterNodeChange},\n\t}\n}\n\n// isSchedulableAfterPodChange is invoked whenever a pod deleted or updated. It checks whether\n// that change made a previously unschedulable pod schedulable.\nfunc (f *Fit) isSchedulableAfterPodChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\n\toriginalPod, modifiedPod, err := schedutil.As[*v1.Pod](oldObj, newObj)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.Queue, err\n\t}\n\n\tif modifiedPod == nil {\n\t\tif originalPod.Spec.NodeName == \"\" {\n\t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"the deleted pod was unscheduled and it wouldn't make the unscheduled pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"deletedPod\", klog.KObj(originalPod))\n\t\t\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"another scheduled pod was deleted, and it may make the unscheduled pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"deletedPod\", klog.KObj(originalPod))\n\t\treturn framework.Queue, nil\n\t}\n\n\tif !f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling {\n\t\t// If InPlacePodVerticalScaling (KEP 1287) is disabled, it cannot free up resources.\n\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"another pod was modified, but InPlacePodVerticalScaling is disabled, so it doesn't make the unscheduled pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"modifiedPod\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod))\n\t\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Modifications may or may not be relevant. We only care about modifications that\n\t// change the other pod's resource request and the resource is also requested by the\n\t// pod we are trying to schedule.\n\tif !f.isResourceScaleDown(pod, originalPod, modifiedPod) {\n\t\tif loggerV := logger.V(10); loggerV.Enabled() {\n\t\t\t// Log more information.\n\t\t\tloggerV.Info(\"another Pod got modified, but the modification isn't related to the resource request\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"modifiedPod\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod), \"diff\", cmp.Diff(originalPod, modifiedPod))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"another Pod got modified, but the modification isn't related to the resource request\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"modifiedPod\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod))\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n\t}\n\n\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"the max request resources of another scheduled pod got reduced and it may make the unscheduled pod schedulable\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"modifiedPod\", klog.KObj(modifiedPod))\n\treturn framework.Queue, nil\n}\n\n// isResourceScaleDown checks whether the resource request of the modified pod is less than the original pod\n// for the resources requested by the pod we are trying to schedule.\nfunc (f *Fit) isResourceScaleDown(targetPod, originalOtherPod, modifiedOtherPod *v1.Pod) bool {\n\tif modifiedOtherPod.Spec.NodeName == \"\" {\n\t\t// no resource is freed up whatever the pod is modified.\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// the other pod was scheduled, so modification or deletion may free up some resources.\n\toriginalMaxResourceReq, modifiedMaxResourceReq := &framework.Resource{}, &framework.Resource{}\n\toriginalMaxResourceReq.SetMaxResource(resource.PodRequests(originalOtherPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling}))\n\tmodifiedMaxResourceReq.SetMaxResource(resource.PodRequests(modifiedOtherPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling}))\n\n\t// check whether the resource request of the modified pod is less than the original pod.\n\tpodRequests := resource.PodRequests(targetPod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{InPlacePodVerticalScalingEnabled: f.enableInPlacePodVerticalScaling})\n\tfor rName, rValue := range podRequests {\n\t\tif rValue.IsZero() {\n\t\t\t// We only care about the resources requested by the pod we are trying to schedule.\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tswitch rName {\n\t\tcase v1.ResourceCPU:\n\t\t\tif originalMaxResourceReq.MilliCPU > modifiedMaxResourceReq.MilliCPU {\n\t\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase v1.ResourceMemory:\n\t\t\tif originalMaxResourceReq.Memory > modifiedMaxResourceReq.Memory {\n\t\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase v1.ResourceEphemeralStorage:\n\t\t\tif originalMaxResourceReq.EphemeralStorage > modifiedMaxResourceReq.EphemeralStorage {\n\t\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tif schedutil.IsScalarResourceName(rName) && originalMaxResourceReq.ScalarResources[rName] > modifiedMaxResourceReq.ScalarResources[rName] {\n\t\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// isSchedulableAfterNodeChange is invoked whenever a node added or changed. It checks whether\n// that change made a previously unschedulable pod schedulable.\nfunc (f *Fit) isSchedulableAfterNodeChange(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, oldObj, newObj interface{}) (framework.QueueingHint, error) {\n\t_, modifiedNode, err := schedutil.As[*v1.Node](oldObj, newObj)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.Queue, err\n\t}\n\t// TODO: also check if the original node meets the pod's resource requestments once preCheck is completely removed.\n\t// See: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/110175\n\tif isFit(pod, modifiedNode) {\n\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"node was updated, and may fit with the pod's resource requestments\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(modifiedNode))\n\t\treturn framework.Queue, nil\n\t}\n\n\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"node was created or updated, but it doesn't have enough resource(s) to accommodate this pod\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"node\", klog.KObj(modifiedNode))\n\treturn framework.QueueSkip, nil\n}\n\n// isFit checks if the pod fits the node. If the node is nil, it returns false.\n// It constructs a fake NodeInfo object for the node and checks if the pod fits the node.\nfunc isFit(pod *v1.Pod, node *v1.Node) bool {\n\tif node == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tnodeInfo := framework.NewNodeInfo()\n\tnodeInfo.SetNode(node)\n\treturn len(Fits(pod, nodeInfo)) == 0\n}\n\n// Filter invoked at the filter extension point.\n// Checks if a node has sufficient resources, such as cpu, memory, gpu, opaque int resources etc to run a pod.\n// It returns a list of insufficient resources, if empty, then the node has all the resources requested by the pod.\nfunc (f *Fit) Filter(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\ts, err := getPreFilterState(cycleState)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\n\tinsufficientResources := fitsRequest(s, nodeInfo, f.ignoredResources, f.ignoredResourceGroups)\n\n\tif len(insufficientResources) != 0 {\n\t\t// We will keep all failure reasons.\n\t\tfailureReasons := make([]string, 0, len(insufficientResources))\n\t\tfor i := range insufficientResources {\n\t\t\tfailureReasons = append(failureReasons, insufficientResources[i].Reason)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Unschedulable, failureReasons...)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc hasRestartableInitContainer(pod *v1.Pod) bool {\n\tfor _, c := range pod.Spec.InitContainers {\n\t\tif c.RestartPolicy != nil && *c.RestartPolicy == v1.ContainerRestartPolicyAlways {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// InsufficientResource describes what kind of resource limit is hit and caused the pod to not fit the node.\ntype InsufficientResource struct {\n\tResourceName v1.ResourceName\n\t// We explicitly have a parameter for reason to avoid formatting a message on the fly\n\t// for common resources, which is expensive for cluster autoscaler simulations.\n\tReason    string\n\tRequested int64\n\tUsed      int64\n\tCapacity  int64\n}\n\n// Fits checks if node have enough resources to host the pod.\nfunc Fits(pod *v1.Pod, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo) []InsufficientResource {\n\treturn fitsRequest(computePodResourceRequest(pod), nodeInfo, nil, nil)\n}\n\nfunc fitsRequest(podRequest *preFilterState, nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo, ignoredExtendedResources, ignoredResourceGroups sets.Set[string]) []InsufficientResource {\n\tinsufficientResources := make([]InsufficientResource, 0, 4)\n\n\tallowedPodNumber := nodeInfo.Allocatable.AllowedPodNumber\n\tif len(nodeInfo.Pods)+1 > allowedPodNumber {\n\t\tinsufficientResources = append(insufficientResources, InsufficientResource{\n\t\t\tResourceName: v1.ResourcePods,\n\t\t\tReason:       \"Too many pods\",\n\t\t\tRequested:    1,\n\t\t\tUsed:         int64(len(nodeInfo.Pods)),\n\t\t\tCapacity:     int64(allowedPodNumber),\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\tif podRequest.MilliCPU == 0 &&\n\t\tpodRequest.Memory == 0 &&\n\t\tpodRequest.EphemeralStorage == 0 &&\n\t\tlen(podRequest.ScalarResources) == 0 {\n\t\treturn insufficientResources\n\t}\n\n\tif podRequest.MilliCPU > 0 && podRequest.MilliCPU > (nodeInfo.Allocatable.MilliCPU-nodeInfo.Requested.MilliCPU) {\n\t\tinsufficientResources = append(insufficientResources, InsufficientResource{\n\t\t\tResourceName: v1.ResourceCPU,\n\t\t\tReason:       \"Insufficient cpu\",\n\t\t\tRequested:    podRequest.MilliCPU,\n\t\t\tUsed:         nodeInfo.Requested.MilliCPU,\n\t\t\tCapacity:     nodeInfo.Allocatable.MilliCPU,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\tif podRequest.Memory > 0 && podRequest.Memory > (nodeInfo.Allocatable.Memory-nodeInfo.Requested.Memory) {\n\t\tinsufficientResources = append(insufficientResources, InsufficientResource{\n\t\t\tResourceName: v1.ResourceMemory,\n\t\t\tReason:       \"Insufficient memory\",\n\t\t\tRequested:    podRequest.Memory,\n\t\t\tUsed:         nodeInfo.Requested.Memory,\n\t\t\tCapacity:     nodeInfo.Allocatable.Memory,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\tif podRequest.EphemeralStorage > 0 &&\n\t\tpodRequest.EphemeralStorage > (nodeInfo.Allocatable.EphemeralStorage-nodeInfo.Requested.EphemeralStorage) {\n\t\tinsufficientResources = append(insufficientResources, InsufficientResource{\n\t\t\tResourceName: v1.ResourceEphemeralStorage,\n\t\t\tReason:       \"Insufficient ephemeral-storage\",\n\t\t\tRequested:    podRequest.EphemeralStorage,\n\t\t\tUsed:         nodeInfo.Requested.EphemeralStorage,\n\t\t\tCapacity:     nodeInfo.Allocatable.EphemeralStorage,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\tfor rName, rQuant := range podRequest.ScalarResources {\n\t\t// Skip in case request quantity is zero\n\t\tif rQuant == 0 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif v1helper.IsExtendedResourceName(rName) {\n\t\t\t// If this resource is one of the extended resources that should be ignored, we will skip checking it.\n\t\t\t// rName is guaranteed to have a slash due to API validation.\n\t\t\tvar rNamePrefix string\n\t\t\tif ignoredResourceGroups.Len() > 0 {\n\t\t\t\trNamePrefix = strings.Split(string(rName), \"/\")[0]\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif ignoredExtendedResources.Has(string(rName)) || ignoredResourceGroups.Has(rNamePrefix) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif rQuant > (nodeInfo.Allocatable.ScalarResources[rName] - nodeInfo.Requested.ScalarResources[rName]) {\n\t\t\tinsufficientResources = append(insufficientResources, InsufficientResource{\n\t\t\t\tResourceName: rName,\n\t\t\t\tReason:       fmt.Sprintf(\"Insufficient %v\", rName),\n\t\t\t\tRequested:    podRequest.ScalarResources[rName],\n\t\t\t\tUsed:         nodeInfo.Requested.ScalarResources[rName],\n\t\t\t\tCapacity:     nodeInfo.Allocatable.ScalarResources[rName],\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn insufficientResources\n}\n\n// Score invoked at the Score extension point.\nfunc (f *Fit) Score(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\n\tnodeInfo, err := preScoreStateKey = \"PreScore\" + Name\nconst invalidScore = -1\n\n// preScoreState computed at PreScore and used at Score.\n// Fields are exported for comparison during testing.\ntype preScoreState topology.\n\t// This allows the pod counts of smaller topologies to not be watered down by\n\t// bigger ones.\n\tTopologyNormalizingWeight []float64\n}\n\n// Clone implements the mandatory Clone interface. out the nodes which\n// don't have required topologyKey(s), and initialize:\n// 1) s.TopologyPairToPodCounts: keyed with both eligible topology pair and node names.\n// 2) s.IgnoredNodes: the set of fmt.Errorf(\"obtaining pod's soft topology spread constraints: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\ts.Constraints, err = pl.buildDefaultConstraints(pod, v1.ScheduleAnyway)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"setting default soft topology spread constraints: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif len(s.Constraints) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\ttopoSize := make([]int, len(s.Constraints))\n\tfor _, node := range filteredNodes {\n\t\tif requireAllTopologies && !nodeLabelsMatchSpreadConstraints(node.Node().Labels, s.Constraints) {\n\t\t\t// Nodes which don't have all required topologyKeys present are ignored\n\t\t\t// when scoring later.\n\t\t\ts.IgnoredNodes.Insert(node.Node().Name)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor i, constraint := range s.Constraints {\n\t\t\t// per-node counts are calculated during Score.\n\t\t\tif constraint.TopologyKey == v1.LabelHostname {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tpair := topologyPair{key: constraint.TopologyKey, value: node.Node().Labels[constraint.TopologyKey]}\n\t\t\tif s.TopologyPairToPodCounts[pair] == nil {\n\t\t\t\ts.TopologyPairToPodCounts[pair] = new(int64)\n\t\t\t\ttopoSize[i]++\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\ts.TopologyNormalizingWeight = make([]float64, len(s.Constraints))\n\tfor i, c := range s.Constraints {\n\t\tsz := topoSize[i]\n\t\tif c.TopologyKey == v1.LabelHostname {\n\t\t\tsz = len(filteredNodes) - len(s.IgnoredNodes)\n\t\t}\n\t\ts.TopologyNormalizingWeight[i] = topologyNormalizingWeight(sz)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// PreScore builds and writes cycle state used by Score and NormalizeScore.\nfunc (pl *PodTopologySpread) PreScore(\n\tctx context.Context,\n\tcycleState *framework.CycleState,\n\tpod *v1.Pod,\n\tfilteredNodes []*framework.NodeInfo,\n) *framework.Status {\n\tallNodes, err := pl.sharedLister.NodeInfos().List()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"getting all nodes: %w\", err))\n\t}\n\n\tif len(allNodes) == 0 {\n\t\t// No need to score.\n\t\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Skip)\n\t}\n\n\tstate := &preScoreState{\n\t\tIgnoredNodes:            sets.New[string](),\n\t\tTopologyPairToPodCounts: make(map[topologyPair]*int64),\n\t}\n\t// Only require that nodes have all the topology labels if using\n\t// non-system-default spreading rules. This allows nodes that don't have a\n\t// zone label to still have hostname spreading.\n\trequireAllTopologies := len(pod.Spec.TopologySpreadConstraints) > 0 || !pl.systemDefaulted\n\terr = pl.initPreScoreState(state, pod, filteredNodes, requireAllTopologies)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"calculating preScoreState: %w\", err))\n\t}\n\n\t// return Skip if incoming pod doesn't have soft topology spread Constraints.\n\tif len(state.Constraints) == 0 {\n\t\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Skip)\n\t}\n\n\t// Ignore parsing errors for backwards compatibility.\n\trequiredNodeAffinity := nodeaffinity.GetRequiredNodeAffinity(pod)\n\tprocessAllNode := func(i int) {\n\t\tnodeInfo := allNodes[i]\n\t\tnode := nodeInfo.Node()\n\n\t\tif !pl.enableNodeInclusionPolicyInPodTopologySpread {\n\t\t\t// `node` should satisfy incoming pod's NodeSelector/NodeAffinity\n\t\t\tif match, _ := requiredNodeAffinity.Match(node); !match {\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// All topologyKeys need to be present in `node`\n\t\tif requireAllTopologies && !nodeLabelsMatchSpreadConstraints(node.Labels, state.Constraints) {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, c := range state.Constraints {\n\t\t\tif pl.enableNodeInclusionPolicyInPodTopologySpread &&\n\t\t\t\t!c.matchNodeInclusionPolicies(pod, node, requiredNodeAffinity) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tpair := topologyPair{key: c.TopologyKey, value: node.Labels[c.TopologyKey]}\n\t\t\t// If current topology pair is not associated with any candidate node,\n\t\t\t// continue to avoid unnecessary calculation.\n\t\t\t// Per-node counts are also skipped, as they are done during Score.\n\t\t\ttpCount := state.TopologyPairToPodCounts[pair]\n\t\t\tif tpCount == nil {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcount := countPodsMatchSelector(nodeInfo.Pods, c.Selector, pod.Namespace)\n\t\t\tatomic.AddInt64(tpCount, int64(count))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tpl.parallelizer.Until(ctx, len(allNodes), processAllNode, pl.Name())\n\n\tcycleState.Write(preScoreStateKey, state)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Score invoked at the Score extension point.\n// The \"score\" returned in this function is the matching number of pods on the `nodeName`,\n// it is normalized later.\nfunc (pl *PodTopologySpread) Score(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\n\tnodeInfo, err := pl.sharedLister.NodeInfos().Get(nodeName)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"getting node %q from Snapshot: %w\", nodeName, err))\n\t}\n\n\tnode := nodeInfo.Node()\n\ts, err := getPreScoreState(cycleState)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Return if the node is not qualified.\n\tif s.IgnoredNodes.Has(node.Name) {\n\t\treturn 0, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// For each present <pair>, current node gets a credit of <matchSum>.\n\t// And we sum up <matchSum> and return it as this node's score.\n\tvar score float64\n\tfor i, c := range s.Constraints {\n\t\tif tpVal, ok := node.Labels[c.TopologyKey]; ok {\n\t\t\tvar cnt int64\n\t\t\tif c.TopologyKey == v1.LabelHostname {\n\t\t\t\tcnt = int64(countPodsMatchSelector(nodeInfo.Pods, c.Selector, pod.Namespace))\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tpair := topologyPair{key: c.TopologyKey, value: tpVal}\n\t\t\t\tcnt = *s.TopologyPairToPodCounts[pair]\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tscore += scoreForCount(cnt, c.MaxSkew, s.TopologyNormalizingWeight[i])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn int64(math.Round(score)), nil\n}\n\n// NormalizeScore invoked after scoring all nodes.\nfunc (pl *PodTopologySpread) NormalizeScore(ctx context.Context, cycleState *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, scores framework.NodeScoreList) *framework.Status {\n\ts, err := getPreScoreState(cycleState)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\tif s == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate <minScore> and <maxScore>\n\tvar minScore int64 = math.MaxInt64\n\tvar maxScore int64\n\tfor i, score := range scores {\n\t\t// it's mandatory to check if <score.Name> is present in m.IgnoredNodes\n\t\tif s.IgnoredNodes.Has(score.Name) {\n\t\t\tscores[i].Score = invalidScore\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif score.Score < minScore {\n\t\t\tminScore = score.Score\n\t\t}\n\t\tif score.Score > maxScore {\n\t\t\tmaxScore = score.Score\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tfor i := range scores {\n\t\tif scores[i].Score == invalidScore {\n\t\t\tscores[i].Score = 0\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif maxScore == 0 {\n\t\t\tscores[i].Score = framework.MaxNodeScore\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\ts := scores[i].Score\n\t\tscores[i].Score = framework.MaxNodeScore * (maxScore + minScore - s) / maxScore\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// ScoreExtensions of the Score plugin.\nfunc (pl *PodTopologySpread) ScoreExtensions() framework.ScoreExtensions {\n\treturn pl\n}\n\nfunc getPreScoreState(cycleState *framework.CycleState) (*preScoreState, error) {\n\tc, err := cycleState.Read(preScoreStateKey)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error reading %q from cycleState: %w\", preScoreStateKey, err)\n\t}\n\n\ts, ok := c.(*preScoreState)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"%+v  convert to podtopologyspread.preScoreState error\", c)\n\t}\n\treturn s, nil\n}\n\n// topologyNormalizingWeight calculates the weight for the topology, based on\n// the number of values that exist for a topology.\n// Since <size> is at least 1 (all nodes that passed the Filters are in the\n// same topology), and k8s supports 5k nodes, the result is in the interval\n// <1.09, 8.52>.\n//\n// Note: <size> could also be zero when no nodes have the required topologies,\n// however we don't care about topology weight in this case as we return a 0\n// score for all nodes.\nfunc topologyNormalizingWeight(size int) float64 {\n\treturn math.Log(float64(size + 2))\n}\n\n// scoreForCount calculates the score based on number of matching pods in a\n// topology domain, the constraint's maxSkew and the topology weight.\n// `maxSkew-1` is added to the score so that differences between topology\n// domains get watered down, controlling the tolerance of the score to skews.\nfunc scoreForCount(cnt int64, maxSkew int32, tpWeight float64) float64 {\n\treturn float64(cnt)*tpWeight + float64(maxSkew-1)\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":265,"oldLines":8,"newStart":265,"newLines":8,"lines":["   return nil"," }"," ","-// ScoreExtensions of the Score plugin.","-func (pl They correspond to a reasonable size range for\n// container images compressed and stored in registries; 90%ile of images on dockerhub drops into this range.\nconst (\n\tmb                    int64 = 1024 * 1024\n\tminThreshold          int64 = 23 * mb\n\tmaxContainerThreshold int64 = 1000 * mb\n)\n\n// ImageLocality is a score plugin that favors nodes that already have requested pod container's images.\ntype ImageLocality struct {\n\thandle framework.Handle\n}\n\nvar _ framework.ScorePlugin = &ImageLocality{}\n\n// Name is the name of the plugin used in the plugin registry and configurations.\nconst Name = names.ImageLocality\n\n// Name returns name of the plugin. It is used in logs, etc.\nfunc (pl *ImageLocality) Name() string {\n\treturn Name\n}\n\n// Score invoked at the score extension point.\nfunc (pl *ImageLocality) Score(ctx context.Context, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\n\tnodeInfo, err := pl.handle.SnapshotSharedLister().NodeInfos().Get(nodeName)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(fmt.Errorf(\"getting node %q from Snapshot: %w\", nodeName, err))\n\t}\n\n\tnodeInfos, err := pl.handle.SnapshotSharedLister().NodeInfos().List()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, framework.AsStatus(err)\n\t}\n\ttotalNumNodes := len(nodeInfos)\n\n\timageScores := sumImageScores(nodeInfo, pod, totalNumNodes)\n\tscore := calculatePriority(imageScores, len(pod.Spec.InitContainers)+len(pod.Spec.Containers))\n\n\treturn score, nil\n}\n\n// ScoreExtensions of the Score plugin.\nfunc (pl *ImageLocality) ScoreExtensions() framework.ScoreExtensions {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// New initializes a new plugin and returns it.\nfunc New(_ context.Context, _ runtime.Object, h framework.Handle) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n\treturn &ImageLocality{handle: h}, nil\n}\n\n// calculatePriority returns the priority of a node. Given the sumScores of requested images on the node, the node's\n// priority is obtained by scaling the maximum priority value with a ratio proportional to the sumScores.\nfunc calculatePriority(sumScores int64, numContainers int) int64 {\n\tmaxThreshold := maxContainerThreshold * int64(numContainers)\n\tif sumScores < minThreshold {\n\t\tsumScores = minThreshold\n\t} else if sumScores > maxThreshold {\n\t\tsumScores = maxThreshold\n\t}\n\n\treturn framework.MaxNodeScore * (sumScores - minThreshold) / (maxThreshold - minThreshold)\n}\n\n// sumImageScores returns the sum of image scores of all the containers that are already on the node.\n// Each image receives a raw score of its size, scaled by scaledImageScore. The raw scores are later used to calculate\n// the final score.\nfunc sumImageScores(nodeInfo *framework.NodeInfo, pod *v1.Pod, totalNumNodes int) int64 {\n\tvar sum int64\n\tfor _, container := range pod.Spec.InitContainers {\n\t\tif state, ok := nodeInfo.ImageStates[normalizedImageName(container.Image)]; ok {\n\t\t\tsum += scaledImageScore(state, totalNumNodes)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tfor _, container := range pod.Spec.Containers {\n\t\tif state, ok := nodeInfo.ImageStates[normalizedImageName(container.Image)]; ok {\n\t\t\tsum += scaledImageScore(state, totalNumNodes)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn sum\n}\n\n// scaledImageScore returns an adaptively scaled score for the given state of an image.\n// The size of the image is used as the base score, scaled by a factor which considers how much nodes the image has \"spread\" to.\n// This heuristic aims to mitigate the undesirable \"node heating problem\", i.e., pods get assigned to the same or\n// a few nodes due to image locality.\nfunc scaledImageScore(imageState *framework.ImageStateSummary, totalNumNodes int) int64 {\n\tspread := float64(imageState.NumNodes) / float64(totalNumNodes)\n\treturn int64(float64(imageState.Size) * spread)\n}\n\n// normalizedImageName returns the CRI compliant name for a given image.\n// TODO: cover the corner cases of missed matches, e.g,\n// 1. extenderPDBViolations, fits, err := f.selectVictimsOnNodeByExtender(logger, pod, nodeInfo)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\t// If it's unfit after extender's preemption, this node is unresolvable by preemption overall,\n\t\t// let's remove it from potential preemption nodes.\n\t\tif !fits {\n\t\t\tdelete(nodeNameToVictimsCopy, nodeName)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Append new + int64(extenderPDBViolations)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nodeNameToVictimsCopy, nil\n}\n\n// selectVictimsOnNodeByExtender checks the given nodes->pods map with predicates on extender's side.\n// Returns:\n// 1. More victim pods (if any) amended by preemption phase of extender.\n// 2. Number of violating victim (used to calculate PDB).\n// 3. Fits or not after preemption phase on extender's side.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) selectVictimsOnNodeByExtender(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, node *framework.NodeInfo) ([]*v1.Pod, int, bool, error) {\n\t// If a extender support preemption but have no cached node info, let's run filter to make sure\n\t// default scheduler's decision still stand with given pod and node.\n\tif !f.NodeCacheCapable {\n\t\terr := f.runPredicate(pod, node)\n\t\tif err.IsSuccess() {\n\t\t\treturn []*v1.Pod{}, 0, true, nil\n\t\t} else if err.IsRejected() {\n\t\t\treturn nil, 0, false, nil\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\treturn nil, 0, false, err.AsError()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Otherwise, as a extender support preemption and have cached node info, we will assume cachedNodeNameToInfo is available\n\t// and get cached node info by given node name.\n\tnodeInfoCopy := f.CachedNodeNameToInfo[node.Node().Name].Snapshot()\n\n\tvar potentialVictims []*v1.Pod\n\n\tremovePod := func(rp *v1.Pod) error {\n\t\treturn nodeInfoCopy.RemovePod(logger, rp)\n\t}\n\taddPod := func(ap *v1.Pod) {\n\t\tnodeInfoCopy.AddPod(ap)\n\t}\n\t// As the first step, remove all the lower priority pods from the node and\n\t// check if the given pod can be scheduled.\n\tpodPriority := corev1helpers.PodPriority(pod)\n\tfor _, p := range nodeInfoCopy.Pods {\n\t\tif corev1helpers.PodPriority(p.Pod) < podPriority {\n\t\t\tpotentialVictims = append(potentialVictims, p.Pod)\n\t\t\tif err := removePod(p.Pod); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, 0, false, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tsort.Slice(potentialVictims, func(i, j int) bool { return util.MoreImportantPod(potentialVictims[i], potentialVictims[j]) })\n\n\t// If the new pod does not fit after removing all the lower priority pods,\n\t// we are almost done and this node is not suitable for preemption.\n\tstatus := f.runPredicate(pod, nodeInfoCopy)\n\tif status.IsSuccess() {\n\t\t// pass\n\t} else if status.IsRejected() {\n\t\t// does not fit\n\t\treturn nil, 0, false, nil\n\t} else {\n\t\t// internal errors\n\t\treturn nil, 0, false, status.AsError()\n\t}\n\n\tvar victims []*v1.Pod\n\n\t// TODO(harry): handle PDBs in the future.\n\tnumViolatingVictim := 0\n\n\treprievePod := func(p *v1.Pod) bool {\n\t\taddPod(p)\n\t\tstatus := f.runPredicate(pod, nodeInfoCopy)\n\t\tif !status.IsSuccess() {\n\t\t\tif err := removePod(p); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tvictims = append(victims, p)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn status.IsSuccess()\n\t}\n\n\t// For now, assume all potential victims to be non-violating.\n\t// Now we try to reprieve non-violating victims.\n\tfor _, p := range potentialVictims {\n\t\treprievePod(p)\n\t}\n\n\treturn victims, numViolatingVictim, true, nil\n}\n\n// runPredicate run predicates of extender one by one for given pod and node.\n// Returns: fits or not.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) runPredicate(pod *v1.Pod, node *framework.NodeInfo) *framework.Status {\n\tfor _, predicate := range f.Predicates {\n\t\tstatus := predicate(pod, node)\n\t\tif !status.IsSuccess() {\n\t\t\treturn status\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn framework.NewStatus(framework.Success)\n}\n\n// Filter implements the extender Filter function.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) Filter(pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*framework.NodeInfo) ([]*framework.NodeInfo, extenderv1.FailedNodesMap, extenderv1.FailedNodesMap, error) {\n\tvar filtered []*framework.NodeInfo\n\tfailedNodesMap := extenderv1.FailedNodesMap{}\n\tfailedAndUnresolvableMap := extenderv1.FailedNodesMap{}\n\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n\t\tstatus := f.runPredicate(pod, node)\n\t\tif status.IsSuccess() {\n\t\t\tfiltered = append(filtered, node)\n\t\t} else if status.Code() == framework.Unschedulable {\n\t\t\tfailedNodesMap[node.Node().Name] = fmt.Sprintf(\"FakeExtender: node %q failed\", node.Node().Name)\n\t\t} else if status.Code() == framework.UnschedulableAndUnresolvable {\n\t\t\tfailedAndUnresolvableMap[node.Node().Name] = f.NodeCacheCapable {\n\t\treturn filtered, failedNodesMap, failedAndUnresolvableMap, nil\n\t}\n\treturn filtered, failedNodesMap, failedAndUnresolvableMap, nil\n}\n\n// Prioritize implements the extender Prioritize function.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) Prioritize(pod *v1.Pod, nodes []*framework.NodeInfo) (*extenderv1.HostPriorityList, int64, error) {\n\tresult := extenderv1.HostPriorityList{}\n\tcombinedScores := map[string]int64{}\n\tfor _, prioritizer := range f.Prioritizers {\n\t\tweight := prioritizer.Weight\n\t\tif weight == 0 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tpriorityFunc := prioritizer.Function\n\t\tprioritizedList, err := priorityFunc(pod, nodes)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn &extenderv1.HostPriorityList{}, 0, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor _, hostEntry := range *prioritizedList {\n\t\t\tcombinedScores[hostEntry.Name] += hostEntry.Score * weight\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tfor host, score := range combinedScores {\n\t\tresult = append(result, extenderv1.HostPriority{Host: host, Score: score})\n\t}\n\treturn &result, f.Weight, nil\n}\n\n// Bind implements the extender Bind function.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) Bind(binding *v1.Binding) error {\n\tif f.Binder != nil {\n\t\treturn f.Binder()\n\t}\n\tif len(f.FilteredNodes) != 0 {\n\t\tfor _, node := range f.FilteredNodes {\n\t\t\tif node.Node().Name == binding.Target.Name {\n\t\t\t\tf.FilteredNodes = nil\n\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\terr := fmt.Errorf(\"Node %v not in filtered nodes %v\", binding.Target.Name, f.FilteredNodes)\n\t\tf.FilteredNodes = nil\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// IsBinder returns true indicating the extender implements the Binder function.\nfunc (f *FakeExtender) IsBinder() bool {\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// cancel()\n\n\tvar (\n\t\tinitialNodeNumber        = 1000\n\t\tinitialPodNumber         = 500\n\t\twaitSchedulingPodNumber  = 200\n\t\tdeleteNodeNumberPerRound = 20\n\t\tcreatePodNumberPerRound  = \"FakeNodeSelectorDependOnPodAnnotation\"}}},\n\t\t\t\t\tQueueSort: schedulerapi.PluginSet{Enabled: []schedulerapi.Plugin{{Name: \"PrioritySort\"}}},\n\t\t\t\t\tBind: fmt.Sprintf(\"node%d\", deleteNodeIndex)\n\t\t\tif err := client.CoreV1().Nodes().Delete(ctx, deleteNodeName, metav1.DeleteOptions{}); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdeleteNodeIndex++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tvar createPodIndex int\n\tcreatePodsOneRound := func() {\n\t\tif createPodIndex > waitSchedulingPodNumber {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < createPodNumberPerRound; i++ {\n\t\t\tpodName := fmt.Sprintf(\"pod%d\", createPodIndex)\n\t\t\t// Note: the node(specifiedNodeName) may already be deleted, which leads pod scheduled failed.\n\t\t\tspecifiedNodeName := fmt.Sprintf(\"node%d\", random.Intn(initialNodeNumber))\n\n\t\t\twaitSchedulingPod := st.MakePod().Namespace(fakeNamespace).Name(podName).UID(podName).Annotation(fakeSpecifiedNodeNameAnnotation, specifiedNodeName).SchedulerName(fakeSchedulerName).Obj()\n\t\t\tif _, err := client.CoreV1().Pods(fakeNamespace).Create(ctx, waitSchedulingPod, metav1.CreateOptions{}); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcreatePodIndex++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Following we start 2 goroutines asynchronously to detect potential racing issues:\n\t// 1) One is responsible for deleting several nodes in each round;\n\t// 2) Another is creating several pods in each round to trigger scheduling;\n\t// Those two goroutines will stop until ctx.Done() is called, which means all waiting pods are scheduled at least once.\n\tgo wait.Until(deleteNodesOneRound, 10*time.Millisecond, ctx.Done())\n\tgo wait.Until(createPodsOneRound, 9*time.Millisecond, ctx.Done())\n\n\t// Capture the events to wait all pods to be scheduled at least once.\n\tallWaitSchedulingPods := sync.WaitGroup\n\twg.Add(waitSchedulingPodNumber)\n\tstopFn, err := broadcaster.StartEventWatcher(func(obj runtime.Object) {\n\t\te, ok := obj.(*eventsv1.Event)\n\t\tif !ok || (e.Reason != \"Scheduled\" && e.Reason != \"FailedScheduling\") {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tif allWaitSchedulingPods.Has(e.Regarding.Name) {\n\t\t\twg.Done()\n\t\t\tallWaitSchedulingPods.Delete(e.Regarding.Name)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\tdefer stopFn()\n\n\twg.Wait()\n}\n\nfunc TestSchedulerScheduleOne(t *framework.QueuedPodInfo, status *framework.Status, _ *framework.NominatingInfo, _ time.Time) {\n\t\t\t\tgotPod = p.Pod\n\t\t\t\tgotError = status.AsError()\n\n\t\t\t\tmsg := truncateMessage(gotError.Error())\n\t\t\t\tfwk.EventRecorder().Eventf(p.Pod, nil, v1.EventTypeWarning, \"FailedScheduling\", \"Scheduling\", msg)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcalled := make(chan struct{})\n\t\t\tstopFunc, err := eventBroadcaster.StartEventWatcher(func(obj runtime.Object) {\n\t\t\t\te, _ := obj.(*eventsv1.Event)\n\t\t\t\tif e.Reason != item.eventReason {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"got event %v, want %v\", e.Reason, item.eventReason)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tclose(called)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tsched.ScheduleOne(ctx)\n\t\t\t<-called\n\t\t\tif e, a := item.expectAssumedPod, gotAssumedPod; !reflect.DeepEqual(e, a) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"assumed pod: wanted %v, got %v\", e, a)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif e, a := item.expectErrorPod, gotPod; !reflect.DeepEqual(e, a) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"error pod: wanted %v, got %v\", e, a)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif e, a := item.expectForgetPod, gotForgetPod; !reflect.DeepEqual(e, a) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"forget pod: wanted %v, got %v\", e, a)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif e, a := item.expectError, gotError; !reflect.DeepEqual(e, a) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"error: wanted %v, got %v\", e, a)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif diff := cmp.Diff(item.expectBind, gotBinding); diff != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"got binding diff (-want, +got): %s\", diff)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tstopFunc()\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestSchedulerNoPhantomPodAfterExpire(t *testing.T) {\n\tlogger, ctx := ktesting.NewTestContext(t)\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\tqueuedPodStore := clientcache.NewFIFO(clientcache.MetaNamespaceKeyFunc)\n\tscache := internalcache.New(ctx, 100*time.Millisecond)\n\tpod := podWithPort(\"pod.Name\", \"\", 8080)\n\tnode := v1.Node{ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: \"node1\", UID: types.UID(\"node1\")}}\n\tscache.AddNode(logger, &node)\n\n\tfns := []tf.RegisterPluginFunc{\n\t\ttf.RegisterQueueSortPlugin(queuesort.Name, queuesort.New),\n\t\ttf.RegisterBindPlugin(defaultbinder.Name, defaultbinder.New),\n\t\ttf.RegisterPluginAsExtensions(nodeports.Name, nodeports.New, \"Filter\", \"PreFilter\"),\n\t}\n\tscheduler, bindingChan, errChan := setupTestSchedulerWithOnePodOnNode(ctx, t, queuedPodStore, scache, pod, &node, fns...)\n\n\twaitPodExpireChan := make(chan struct{})\n\ttimeout := make(chan struct{})\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tfor {\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-timeout:\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tpods, err := scache.PodCount()\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\terrChan <- fmt.Errorf(\"cache.List failed: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif pods == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tclose(waitPodExpireChan)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ttime.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\t// waiting for the assumed pod to expire\n\tselect {\n\tcase err := <-errChan:\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\tcase <-waitPodExpireChan:\n\tcase <-time.After(wait.ForeverTestTimeout):\n\t\tclose(timeout)\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"timeout timeout in waiting pod expire after %v\", wait.ForeverTestTimeout)\n\t}\n\n\t// We use conflicted pod ports to incur fit predicate failure if first pod not removed.\n\tsecondPod := podWithPort(\"bar\", \"\", 8080)\n\tqueuedPodStore.Add(secondPod)\n\tscheduler.ScheduleOne(ctx)\n\tselect {\n\tcase b := <-bindingChan:\n\t\texpectBinding := &v1.Binding{\n\t\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: \"bar\", UID: types.UID(\"bar\")},\n\t\t\tTarget:     v1.ObjectReference{Kind: \"Node\", Name: node.Name},\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(expectBinding, b) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"binding want=%v, get=%v\", expectBinding, b)\n\t\t}\n\tcase <-time.After(wait.ForeverTestTimeout):\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"timeout in binding after %v\", wait.ForeverTestTimeout)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestSchedulerNoPhantomPodAfterDelete(t *testing.T) {\n\tlogger, ctx := ktesting.NewTestContext(t)\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\tqueuedPodStore := clientcache.NewFIFO(clientcache.MetaNamespaceKeyFunc)\n\tscache := internalcache.New(ctx, 10*time.Minute)\n\tfirstPod := podWithPort(\"pod.Name\", \"\", 8080)\n\tnode := v1.Node{ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: \"node1\", UID: types.UID(\"node1\")}}\n\tscache.AddNode(logger, &node)\n\tfns := []tf.RegisterPluginFunc{\n\t\ttf.RegisterQueueSortPlugin(queuesort.Name, queuesort.New),\n\t\ttf.RegisterBindPlugin(defaultbinder.Name, defaultbinder.New),\n\t\ttf.RegisterPluginAsExtensions(nodeports.Name, nodeports.New, \"Filter\", \"PreFilter\"),\n\t}\n\tscheduler, bindingChan, errChan := setupTestSchedulerWithOnePodOnNode(ctx, t, queuedPodStore, scache, firstPod, &node, fns...)\n\n\t// We use conflicted pod ports to incur fit predicate failure.\n\tsecondPod := podWithPort(\"bar\", \"\", 8080)\n\tqueuedPodStore.Add(secondPod)\n\t// queuedPodStore: [bar:8080]\n\t// cache: [(assumed)foo:8080]\n\n\tscheduler.ScheduleOne(ctx)\n\tselect {\n\tcase err := <-errChan:\n\t\texpectErr := &framework.FitError{\n\t\t\tPod:         secondPod,\n\t\t\tNumAllNodes: 1,\n\t\t\tDiagnosis: framework.Diagnosis{\n\t\t\t\tNodeToStatusMap: framework.NodeToStatusMap{\n\t\t\t\t\tnode.Name: framework.NewStatus(framework.Unschedulable, nodeports.ErrReason).WithPlugin(nodeports.Name),\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tUnschedulablePlugins: sets.New(nodeports.Name),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(expectErr, err) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"err want=%v, get=%v\", expectErr, err)\n\t\t}\n\tcase <-time.After(wait.ForeverTestTimeout):\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"timeout in fitting after %v\", wait.ForeverTestTimeout)\n\t}\n\n\t// We mimic the workflow of cache behavior when a pod is removed by user.\n\t// Note: if the schedulernodeinfo timeout would be super short, the first pod would expire\n\t// and would be removed itself (without any explicit actions on schedulernodeinfo). Even in that case,\n\t// explicitly AddPod will as well correct the behavior.\n\tfirstPod.Spec.NodeName = node.Name\n\tif err := scache.AddPod(logger, firstPod); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tif err := scache.RemovePod(logger, firstPod); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tqueuedPodStore.Add(secondPod)\n\tscheduler.ScheduleOne(ctx)\n\tselect {\n\tcase b := <-bindingChan:\n\t\texpectBinding := &v1.Binding{\n\t\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: \"bar\", UID: types.UID(\"bar\")},\n\t\t\tTarget:     v1.ObjectReference{Kind: \"Node\", Name: node.Name},\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(expectBinding, b) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"binding want=%v, get=%v\", expectBinding, b)\n\t\t}\n\tcase <-time.After(wait.ForeverTestTimeout):\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"timeout in binding after %v\", wait.ForeverTestTimeout)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestSchedulerFailedSchedulingReasons(t *testing.T) {\n\tlogger, ctx := ktesting.NewTestContext(t)\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\tqueuedPodStore := clientcache.NewFIFO(clientcache.MetaNamespaceKeyFunc)\n\tscache := internalcache.New(ctx, 10*time.Minute)\n\n\t// Design the baseline for the pods, and we will make nodes that don't fit it later.\n\tvar cpu = int64(4)\n\tvar mem = int64(500)\n\tpodWithTooBigResourceRequests := podWithResources(\"bar\", \"\", v1.ResourceList{\n\t\tv1.ResourceCPU:    *(resource.NewQuantity(cpu, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\tv1.ResourceMemory: *(resource.NewQuantity(mem, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t}, v1.ResourceList{\n\t\tv1.ResourceCPU:    *(resource.NewQuantity(cpu, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\tv1.ResourceMemory: *(resource.NewQuantity(mem, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t})\n\n\t// create several nodes which cannot schedule the above pod\n\tvar nodes []*v1.Node\n\tvar objects []runtime.Object\n\tfor i := 0; i < 100; i++ {\n\t\tuid := fmt.Sprintf(\"node%v\", i)\n\t\tnode := v1.Node{\n\t\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: uid, UID: types.UID(uid)},\n\t\t\tStatus: v1.NodeStatus{\n\t\t\t\tCapacity: v1.ResourceList{\n\t\t\t\t\tv1.ResourceCPU:    *(resource.NewQuantity(cpu/2, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\t\t\t\tv1.ResourceMemory: *(resource.NewQuantity(mem/5, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\t\t\t\tv1.ResourcePods:   *(resource.NewQuantity(10, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tAllocatable: v1.ResourceList{\n\t\t\t\t\tv1.ResourceCPU:    *(resource.NewQuantity(cpu/2, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\t\t\t\tv1.ResourceMemory: *(resource.NewQuantity(mem/5, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\t\t\t\tv1.ResourcePods:   *(resource.NewQuantity(10, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\t\t\t}},\n\t\t}\n\t\tscache.AddNode(logger, &node)\n\t\tnodes = append(nodes, &node)\n\t\tobjects = append(objects, &node)\n\t}\n\n\t// Create expected failure reasons for all the nodes. Hopefully they will get rolled up into a non-spammy summary.\n\tfailedNodeStatues := framework.NodeToStatusMap{}\n\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n\t\tfailedNodeStatues[node.Name] = framework.NewStatus(\n\t\t\tframework.Unschedulable,\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Insufficient %v\", v1.ResourceCPU),\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Insufficient %v\", v1.ResourceMemory),\n\t\t).WithPlugin(noderesources.Name)\n\t}\n\tfns := []tf.RegisterPluginFunc{\n\t\ttf.RegisterQueueSortPlugin(queuesort.Name, queuesort.New),\n\t\ttf.RegisterBindPlugin(defaultbinder.Name, defaultbinder.New),\n\t\ttf.RegisterPluginAsExtensions(noderesources.Name, frameworkruntime.FactoryAdapter(feature.Features{}, noderesources.NewFit), \"Filter\", \"PreFilter\"),\n\t}\n\n\tinformerFactory := informers.NewSharedInformerFactory(clientsetfake.NewSimpleClientset(objects...), 0)\n\tscheduler, _, errChan := setupTestScheduler(ctx, t, queuedPodStore, scache, informerFactory, nil, fns...)\n\n\tqueuedPodStore.Add(podWithTooBigResourceRequests)\n\tscheduler.ScheduleOne(ctx)\n\tselect {\n\tcase err := <-errChan:\n\t\texpectErr := &framework.FitError{\n\t\t\tPod:         podWithTooBigResourceRequests,\n\t\t\tNumAllNodes: len(nodes),\n\t\t\tDiagnosis: framework.Diagnosis{\n\t\t\t\tNodeToStatusMap:      failedNodeStatues,\n\t\t\t\tUnschedulablePlugins: sets.New(noderesources.Name),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(fmt.Sprint(expectErr)) > 150 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"message is too spammy ! %v \", len(fmt.Sprint(expectErr)))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(expectErr, err) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"\\n err \\nWANT=%+v,\\nGOT=%+v\", expectErr, err)\n\t\t}\n\tcase <-time.After(wait.ForeverTestTimeout):\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"timeout after %v\", wait.ForeverTestTimeout)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestSchedulerWithVolumeBinding(t *testing.T) {\n\tfindErr := fmt.Errorf(\"find err\")\n\tassumeErr := fmt.Errorf(\"assume err\")\n\tbindErr := fmt.Errorf(\"bind err\")\n\tclient := clientsetfake.NewSimpleClientset()\n\n\teventBroadcaster := string\n\t\tvolumeBinderConfig *volumebinding.FakeVolumeBinderConfig\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"all bound\",\n\t\t\tvolumeBinderConfig: &volumebinding.FakeVolumeBinderConfig{\n\t\t\t\tAllBound: \"FailedScheduling\",\n\t\t\texpectError:        fmt.Errorf(\"running PreBind plugin %q: %w\", volumebinding.Name, bindErr),\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, item := range table {\n\t\tt.Run(item.name, func(t eventBroadcaster.StartEventWatcher(func(obj runtime.Object) {\n\t\t\t\te, _ := obj.(*eventsv1.Event)\n\t\t\t\tif e, a := item.eventReason, e.Reason; e != a {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected %v, got %v\", e, a)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tclose(eventChan)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ts.ScheduleOne(ctx)\n\t\t\t// Wait for pod to succeed or fail scheduling\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-eventChan:\n\t\t\tcase <-time.After(wait.ForeverTestTimeout):\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"scheduling timeout after %v\", wait.ForeverTestTimeout)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tstopFunc()\n\t\t\t// Wait for scheduling to return an error or succeed binding.\n\t\t\tvar (\n\t\t\t\tgotErr  error\n\t\t\t\tgotBind *v1.Binding\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase gotErr = <-errChan:\n\t\t\tcase gotBind = <-bindingChan:\n\t\t\tcase <-time.After(chanTimeout):\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"did not receive pod binding or error after %v\", chanTimeout)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif item.expectError != nil {\n\t\t\t\tif gotErr == nil || item.expectError.Error() != gotErr.Error() {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"err \\nWANT=%+v,\\nGOT=%+v\", item.expectError, gotErr)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else if gotErr != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"err \\nWANT=%+v,\\nGOT=%+v\", item.expectError, gotErr)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif !cmp.Equal(item.expectPodBind, gotBind) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"err \\nWANT=%+v,\\nGOT=%+v\", item.expectPodBind, gotBind)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif item.expectAssumeCalled != fakeVolumeBinder.AssumeCalled {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expectedAssumeCall %v\", item.expectAssumeCalled)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif item.expectBindCalled != fakeVolumeBinder.BindCalled {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expectedBindCall %v\", item.expectBindCalled)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestSchedulerBinding(t *testing.T) {\n\ttable := []struct {\n\t\tpodName      string\n\t\textenders    []framework.Extender\n\t\twantBinderID int\n\t\tname         string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:    \"the extender is not a binder\",\n\t\t\tpodName: \"pod0\",\n\t\t\textenders: []framework.Extender{\n\t\t\t\t&fakeExtender{isBinder: false, interestedPodName: \"pod0\"},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantBinderID: -1, // default binding.\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:    \"one of the extenders is a binder and interested in pod\",\n\t\t\tpodName: \"pod0\",\n\t\t\textenders: []framework.Extender{\n\t\t\t\t&fakeExtender{isBinder: false, interestedPodName: \"pod0\"},\n\t\t\t\t&fakeExtender{isBinder: true, interestedPodName: \"pod0\"},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantBinderID: 1,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:    \"one of the extenders is a binder, but not interested in pod\",\n\t\t\tpodName: \"pod1\",\n\t\t\textenders: []framework.Extender{\n\t\t\t\t&fakeExtender{isBinder: false, interestedPodName: \"pod1\"},\n\t\t\t\t&fakeExtender{isBinder: true, interestedPodName: \"pod0\"},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantBinderID: -1, // default binding.\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:    \"ignore when extender bind failed\",\n\t\t\tpodName: \"pod1\",\n\t\t\textenders: []framework.Extender{\n\t\t\t\t&fakeExtender{isBinder: true, errBind: true, interestedPodName: \"pod1\", ignorable: true},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantBinderID: -1, // default binding.\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, test := range table {\n\t\tt.Run(test.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tpod := st.MakePod().Name(test.podName).Obj()\n\t\t\tdefaultBound := false\n\t\t\tclient := clientsetfake.NewSimpleClientset(pod)\n\t\t\tclient.PrependReactor(\"create\", \"pods\", func(action clienttesting.Action) (bool, runtime.Object, error) {\n\t\t\t\tif action.GetSubresource() == \"binding\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tdefaultBound = true\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\treturn false, nil, nil\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t_, ctx := ktesting.NewTestContext(t)\n\t\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)\n\t\t\tdefer cancel()\n\t\t\tfwk, err := tf.NewFramework(ctx,\n\t\t\t\t[]tf.RegisterPluginFunc{\n\t\t\t\t\ttf.RegisterQueueSortPlugin(queuesort.Name, queuesort.New),\n\t\t\t\t\ttf.RegisterBindPlugin(defaultbinder.Name, defaultbinder.New),\n\t\t\t\t}, \"\", frameworkruntime.WithClientSet(client), frameworkruntime.WithEventRecorder(&events.FakeRecorder{}))\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tsched := &Scheduler{\n\t\t\t\tExtenders:                test.extenders,\n\t\t\t\tCache:                    internalcache.New(ctx, 100*time.Millisecond),\n\t\t\t\tnodeInfoSnapshot:         nil,\n\t\t\t\tpercentageOfNodesToScore: 0,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tstatus := sched.bind(ctx, fwk, pod, \"node\", nil)\n\t\t\tif !status.IsSuccess() {\n\t\t\t\tt.Error(status.AsError())\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Checking default binding.\n\t\t\tif wantBound := test.wantBinderID == -1; defaultBound != wantBound {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"got bound with default binding: %v, want %v\", defaultBound, wantBound)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Checking extenders binding.\n\t\t\tfor i, ext := range test.extenders {\n\t\t\t\twantBound := i == err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif diff := cmp.Diff(tt.expectedNodes, nodes); diff != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"filtered nodes (-want,+got):\\n%s\", diff)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif diff := nil\n}\n\nfunc TestSchedulerMultipleProfilesScheduling(t *testing.T) {\n\tnodes := {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\twg.Wait()\n\n\t// Verify correct bindings and reporting controllers.\n\tif diff := cmp.Diff(wantBindings, bindings); diff != \"\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"pods were
